name: Budget Handicap Vans LLC
fleet_size_est: 45+ vans in inventory (dealer stock, not operating fleet)
districts_or_customers: Sells pre-owned wheelchair-accessible vans to NEMT companies and families in FL and other states; not a contracted student-transport operator
recent_news: Active used-vehicle dealer; reports having sold 1,000+ wheelchair vans since 2016 (BBB/website, ongoing)
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target. This is a used wheelchair-van dealership (Florida-licensed used car dealer), not a school-bus or student-transportation operator. Disqualified from Summit acquisition pipeline.

name: Sheppard Bus Svc
principals: John Sheppard (President/Owner), Kelly Sheppard (Co-owner); founded by Kenneth Sheppard (1952) and Maria Sheppard
districts_or_customers: Cumberland County NJ and broader southern/central NJ; depots in Bridgeton, Millville, Carmel, Trenton, Mays Landing, Burlington, Southampton; original contract with Fairfield Township Schools (1952); has served Seabrook Schools historically
recent_news: Re-entered school bus market in 1998 after Ken Sheppard sold pupil division to Laidlaw in 1988; reacquired Laidlaw's Deerfield Township terminal in 2008; signed Gatekeeper Systems technology contract (industry release, recent)
fit_notes: Highly attractive tuck-in for Summit: 73-year-old family-owned NJ operator with 600+ vehicles, 7 depots, and second-generation leadership (John & Kelly Sheppard). NJ is a core Summit market; succession dynamics (second-gen owners, no public third-gen visible) merit direct outreach. Prior Laidlaw sale/buyback history shows owners willing to transact.

name: All American School Bus Co
principals: Ray L. Fouche (CEO); affiliated entities include Luray's Travel Ltd., Citywide Transit Inc., Rainbow Transit Inc., and Provost Holdings LP
fleet_size_est: ~50-75 buses (estimated from D&B revenue and NYC DOE operator listings)
districts_or_customers: NYC Department of Education contractor; depot at 2265 Richmond Terrace, Staten Island; ATU Local 1181/1061 unionized workforce
recent_news: Listed as active NYC DOE school-bus operator (NYC Schools bus companies directory); minority-owned business per Labor 411; no public M&A activity identified
website: Not confirmed (no active company website found); operating address 2265 Richmond Ter, Staten Island NY 10302
fit_notes: Plausible NYC tuck-in: small Fouche-family operator with NYC DOE contracts on Staten Island, ATU 1181 unionized (consistent with NYC market). Ray Fouche owns a cluster of related transit entities (Citywide, Rainbow, Luray's, Provost Holdings) that could be acquired as a group. Note: company registered to Long Island City address in D&B records; Staten Island Richmond Terrace likely the depot. Worth a confidential approach via Provost Holdings.

name: B & M Bus Charter Inc
principals: Not publicly identified (no officer info disclosed in NYS filing)
fleet_size_est: Unknown; sub-20 employees suggests very small (likely <15 buses)
districts_or_customers: None publicly identified; entity address is residential/commercial mixed block in Sunset Park, Brooklyn (368 60th St)
recent_news: Incorporated as a NY Business Corporation on December 14, 2022; active status; no news coverage, no FMCSA snapshot prominently linked, no website found
fit_notes: De minimis target. Newly formed (2022) Brooklyn charter entity with no discoverable web presence, no website, no named principals, and no confirmed school-bus contracts. Too small/opaque for Summit to prioritize without phone-based qualification; likely a single-owner charter startup rather than a school-bus contractor.

name: Robert's School Bus
principals: Founded by Robert Iwamoto Sr. (1941); current parent Roberts Hawaii is employee-owned
fleet_size_est: ~900 vehicles across Roberts Hawaii group; Roberts Hawaii School Bus operates 181 of Oahu's 305 DOE routes plus Big Island (Hilo) routes
districts_or_customers: Hawaii State Department of Education (statewide, dominant share); also Hawaiian Monarch Hotel, Magic of Polynesia, and visitor shuttle operations
recent_news: 2013 awarded $21M HI DOE contract for 181/305 Oahu routes; 2024 reporting indicated DOE driver contracts set to expire in October (KITV); Roberts Hawaii is the dominant HI school-bus contractor after litigation history with Laupahoehoe/Central Transportation
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target. Roberts Hawaii School Bus is the Hilo arm of Roberts Hawaii, an employee-owned platform-scale operator (~900 vehicles, 1,400 employees) that dominates the HI DOE contract market and operates tour/hotel/shuttle businesses. Any transaction would be platform-scale, not a regional tuck-in, and the ESOP structure complicates ownership change. Disqualify from tuck-in pipeline; could be tracked as a strategic platform/JV opportunity for I Squared separately.
